name: CONSTRAINT_c
description: Constraint block for continuous systems - enforces algebraic constraints
xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CONSTRAINT_c" blockType="c" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="1" simulationFunctionName="constraint_c" simulationFunctionType="IMPLICIT_C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CONSTRAINT_c">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="1" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <ScilabInteger as="integerParameters" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0"/>
  </ScilabInteger>
  <ScilabDouble as="state" height="1" width="2">
    <data line="0" column="0" realPart="0.0"/>
    <data line="0" column="1" realPart="0.0"/>
  </ScilabDouble>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="60.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: state has 2 elements (unusual for simple block)
No realParameters - uses integerParameters and state only
dependsOnU=0 but dependsOnT=1 - evolves with time but not directly from input
criticalRules: IMPLICIT_C_OR_FORTRAN simulation type requires implicit DAE solver
Used for enforcing algebraic constraints in physical modeling
